page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 3.1 GHz. The instance is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPU hardware, and it operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities but weaker multi-core scaling. The single-core stress-ng and Geekbench scores fall into the average tier, supported by hardware features like AVX-512 VNNI and AES cryptography. However, multi-core benchmarks, such as stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, are positioned in the weak category. Memory bandwidth tests show a significant drop from 213.23 GB/sec for cached small blocks to 26.36 GB/sec for uncached large blocks, reflecting potential bottlenecks under heavy memory-intensive operations.

From a resource density perspective, the ecs.hfr6.xlarge offers a cost-efficient entry point for memory-heavy workloads that do not require high-performance compute scaling or local NVMe storage. Given its weak multi-core performance and database operations benchmarks, it is best suited for moderate memory-intensive applications, lightweight caching layers, text processing, and basic web serving rather than high-throughput databases or complex parallel computing tasks.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor, this x86_64 instance lacks local storage and GPUs, relying on a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show average single-core performance in stress-ng and Geekbench, alongside average text processing and single-threaded compression speeds. However, multi-core performance, database operations, and uncached memory bandwidth are relatively weak. Qualitatively, this instance provides a cost-efficient option for memory-heavy workloads with low compute demands. It is best suited for moderate caching, text processing, and lightweight web serving.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring a high RAM-to-vCPU ratio for workloads requiring moderate single-core performance and dedicated memory resources.

32.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
KVM hypervisor architecture with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Average single-core performance but weaker multi-core scaling
